svg.title: Hamster mini-enter-sleep v1 (scripted)
svg.desc: Mini-mode enter-sleep transition (Do-Not-Disturb variant) for clawd-on-desk / MiniCPM-Desk-Pet, right-edge variant (host mirrors for left edge). Plays ONCE then holds at the mini-sleep rest pose. Entered when mini mode starts while Do-Not-Disturb is on (host: enterSvgState = doNotDisturb ? 'mini-enter-sleep' : 'mini-enter'); the host slides the window to the edge first, then plays this in place. A drowsy hamster burrows out and dozes off: (1) both paws reach from off-screen, slightly past then retract to grab the edge (enter-hands-reach, screen x = master y, paw transforms untouched + tip-clipped); (2) the head emerges — necks down then lifts with an overshoot look about the neck (187,252) via enter-head-roll, then droops to the -7deg sleep tilt; (3) the body slides in (enter-slide) and settles while the eyes fall closed (open-eye ellipses fade out, sleeping arcs fade in) and the brows morph to the relaxed sleep brows lifted away from the lenses. The mouth is the confirmed closed sleeping small-mouth THROUGHOUT — no smile (鹿鹿 2026-06-14). Blushes sit BELOW the sunglasses (鹿鹿 2026-06-14; mini-sleep matched for a seamless handoff). Endpoint is verbatim mini-sleep phase=0 (cheek = master value, sleep arcs/brows/mouth, head roll -7, paw/tie confirmed transforms, all wrappers zeroed) so the host handoff to the mini-sleep loop is seamless. A CSS .duration-probe (animation 1.1s, opacity-only keyframe) lets the host read the asset cycle (getMiniEnterDurationMs) and schedule the mini-sleep switch. Default plays once and holds; ?loop=1 replays the whole entry for demo. svg root keeps pointer-events:none. Exposes window.__miniEnterSleep. Pure paths + soft-lift filter, no image/gradient/fetch (the duration-probe keyframe is opacity-only).
